title: Diokno, Salonga and Teehankee
primary_topics: Claudio Teehankee, Jose Diokno, and Jovito Salonga as legal heroes
sub_topics: Estelito Mendoza reaction to GMA right-to-travel decision
Estrada-Arroyo constructive resignation doctrine (Reynato Puno authorship)
Davide and CJ inhibition from Estrada v. Desierto
Teehankee — Ateneo summa cum laude AB and LLB
Diokno and Salonga tied 1st place 1944 bar (95.3%)
Diokno never finished law school + topped CPA
Salonga UP / Harvard / Yale (master's and doctorate)
Salonga's PCGG appointment 1986 + disbandment of law office
Panganiban Parlade Benitez and Africa law firm dissolution (1995)

stances.claim: The doctrine of constructive resignation should be credited to Justice (later Chief Justice) Reynato Puno who wrote the path-breaking unanimous Estrada-Arroyo decision — Davide and CJ took no part in the 13-0 ruling; any attribution to CJ, whether mockingly or admiringly, 'is simply fake news.'
stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal-attribution correction + use of 'fake news' as authoritative dismissal

stances.claim: Claudio Teehankee was the greatest Ateneo law alumnus of all time — summa cum laude in both AB and LLB from Ateneo, numero uno in the 1940 bar; he merits hero-worship as a model for Ateneo law professors and students.
stances.rhetorical_move: structured-credential argument + admission of hero-worship + open invitation to others

stances.claim: Diokno and Salonga deserve equal esteem — both tied for 1st place in the 1944 bar exam with identical 95.3% grades; Diokno did not finish law school yet topped both the bar and the CPA exam; Salonga earned both Harvard master's and Yale doctorate of laws, 'a rare feat only a few in the world have attained.'
Salonga was in awe of only two lawyers, Diokno and Teehankee — he would caution young CJ to 'prepare our trial brief extra well because Pepe Diokno (or Dingdong Teehankee) is our opponent.'
stances.rhetorical_move: mentor-anecdote with first-name nicknames as authentic-intimacy device

stances.claim: In emulation of Salonga's disbanding his law office upon his 1986 PCGG appointment, CJ dissolved his own law firm (Panganiban, Parlade, Benitez and Africa) upon his 1995 Supreme Court appointment — a feeble attempt 'to live in the penumbra of their great shadows.'

institutions: Panganiban, Parlade, Benitez and Africa (his law firm)
events: 1944 bar examinations (Diokno and Salonga tied #1 at 95.3%)
Salonga's 1986 PCGG appointment and law office disbandment
CJ's 1995 SC appointment and law firm dissolution

signature_phrases: I have said it before and will say it again
the greatest Ateneo law alumnus of all time
to live in the penumbra of their great shadows
Please prepare our trial brief extra well
register_markers: though unworthy / though undeserving (recurring humility phrase)
decision_framework_signals: applies doctrinal-attribution correction as principled-record device (giving credit where due)
uses structured-credential hero-worship as transmission mechanism for legal-historical heroes
frames professional-ethics emulation (disbanding law office) as imitatio of mentors
uses 'penumbra of their great shadows' framing for humble self-positioning relative to legal giants
notable_anecdotes: Salonga as mentor at FEU, boss at his law firm, and lifetime guru, in awe of only two lawyers — Diokno and Teehankee — and warning young CJ to prepare trial briefs 'extra well' when either was opposing counsel
Disbanding his law firm Panganiban, Parlade, Benitez and Africa upon his 1995 SC appointment in emulation of Salonga's 1986 disbanding upon PCGG appointment
Closing Dean Sedfrey Candelaria's discourse as FLP professorial lecturer with an invitation to look up to Teehankee as model
